findings.Description: The destroycontract function can be called by any user to terminate the contract, found in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Complete loss of contract functionality and potential theft of remaining ETH.

findings.Description: The fallback function allows recursive calling through ETH transfers, found in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Potential infinite recursion and token minting abuse.

findings.Description: Inconsistent decimal handling across functions leading to incorrect token amounts, found in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Severely broken token economics and incorrect accounting.

findings.Issue: Unsafe math operations without proper checks
findings.Description: Multiple math operations performed without safe arithmetic checks, found in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Potential overflow/underflow leading to incorrect balances.

findings.Issue: Missing access controls for privileged functions
findings.Description: Critical functions lack proper owner restrictions, found in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Unauthorized contract destruction or token manipulation.

findings.Description: Approve/transferFrom pattern vulnerable to allowance race conditions, found in both the smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
Airdrop function lacks proper validation and visibility specifier, found in the smart contract code. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Potential failed airdrops or improper token transfers.

findings.Description: Burn events emit scaled values while operations use raw amounts, found in the smart contract code. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Inconsistent event logging affecting off-chain tracking.

findings.Description: msg.value multiplication in fallback could overflow, found in the smart contract code. This issue appears in the auditing result.
